top_signals.title: Why South Korea’s Won Is Weak Despite An AI-Driven Stock Boom
top_signals.summary: A global AI boom has turbocharged demand for chips from South Korea’s tech giants, including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., helping to drive the country’s benchmark Kospi stock index up more than 150% over the past year. Yet despite the Kospi outpacing every other major equity market in the world, the South Korean won remains one of Asia’s weakest currencies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Trump says willing to wait for a few days to get ’right answer’ on Iran peace deal
top_signals.summary: The ceasefire has yielded little progress, and soaring gasoline prices continue to ​weigh on the president's approval ratings.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
